cross_scene_arcs.title: From Unemployed Dreamer to Reluctant Corporate Insider
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Barney transitions from joblessness and societal marginalization to a formal role in corporate management, symbolizing a shift in social and economic status. Initially dismissed as an impractical inventor, he gradually gains institutional legitimacy through association with Fred and the Fibrerock venture, culminating in his integration into the executive tier at the quarry.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Barney is unemployed and socially undervalued, clinging to an identity as an inventor without recognition or stability.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Barney assumes a white-collar executive role, marked by a suit, office, and managerial responsibilities, though he struggles with the ethical weight of his new position.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Barney's internal conflict about his complicity in corporate decisions that harm workers remains unresolved, questioning whether his rise in status comes at the cost of his empathy and integrity.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Erosion and Restoration of Fred and Barney's Friendship
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Barney's bond with Fred undergoes repeated strain due to deception, loyalty conflicts, and emotional distance, yet persists through moments of crisis and reconciliation. The arc traces their friendship from moments of shared rebellion to betrayal and silence, ultimately culminating in a physical and emotional reunion that reaffirms their deep connection.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Barney begins to question his identity under Fred's pressure to conform, showing early signs of relational imbalance.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: After a period of emotional isolation and unreciprocated loyalty, Barney and Fred reconcile through mutual vulnerability, restoring their bond.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The power imbalance in their friendship—where Fred often takes credit or makes decisions unilaterally—remains unaddressed, leaving the long-term equity of their relationship uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Barney's Moral Awakening and Ethical Dilemma in Power
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: As Barney ascends into positions of influence, he increasingly confronts moral conflicts between loyalty to Fred and his own conscience. He shifts from a passive observer to an active moral agent, warning of dangers, exposing sabotage, and empathizing with displaced workers, even when it puts him at odds with authority.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Barney intervenes to prevent physical danger at the quarry, showing early signs of ethical leadership.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Barney attempts to expose corporate negligence regarding Fibrerock, only to be silenced and removed—a moment that crystallizes his role as a truth-teller in a corrupt system.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Barney's warnings go unheeded, and he is punished for speaking up, raising the question of whether integrity can survive in a system built on deception and power.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Accidental Inventor: Recognition, Erasure, and Reclamation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Barney's journey as an unrecognized inventor begins with personal ambition, suffers erasure when Fred takes credit for Fibrerock, gains partial restitution through financial stake, and culminates in his pivotal role in saving Slate City using his knowledge of coo-coo berry resin—reclaiming his identity as the true problem-solver.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Barney defines himself as an inventor despite lack of success or support, seeking validation outside traditional work.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Barney uses his forgotten expertise to recognize the danger to Slate City and takes decisive action to save it, proving his inventive insight was valid all along.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Despite his critical contributions, Barney remains in Fred's shadow publicly, with no formal recognition for his role in either Fibrerock or the city's salvation.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Loyalty Under Fire: Barney as the Moral Center Amid Collapse
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Throughout the story's climax, Barney consistently acts as the emotional and moral anchor—supporting Fred in breakdown, warning of group violence, confronting villains, and ensuring the safety of children. His arc blends personal loyalty, ethical courage, and physical bravery, positioning him as the quiet hero amidst spectacle and ego.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Barney is the only one to respond to Fred's emotional collapse, showing deep empathy and loyalty.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Barney plays a crucial role in the final rescue, saving Bam-Bam and surviving the collapse, embodying responsibility and care even in chaos.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Despite his heroism, Barney remains underappreciated, and the systemic issues that led to the crisis—corruption, inequality, misrecognition—are not fully resolved or reformed.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Rise and Fall of Fred's Authority
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Fred ascends from a struggling worker to a celebrated executive and Lodge leader, only to lose credibility and social standing after failing to protect his peers and being rejected by the community. His status arc traces a classic rise-and-fall trajectory driven by professional success, moral compromise, and public disillusionment.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Fred gains influence and respect at Slate Construction, mentoring Barney and positioning himself as a leader.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Fred is publicly humiliated, politically defeated, and isolated after the Lodge blames him for the layoffs and elects Piltdown instead.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The workers remain without a raise, and Fred's legacy as a leader is tarnished, leaving his future role in the community uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Fred and Barney: Loyalty, Ego, and Reconciliation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Fred's relationship with Barney evolves from mentorship to manipulation, near-betrayal, and ultimately redemption. Fred repeatedly takes credit for Barney's contributions and wavers in loyalty under pressure, but a moment of emotional collapse leads to a sincere apology and reconciliation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Fred tries to guide Barney toward responsibility, positioning himself as the wiser friend.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: After alienating Barney through pretense and self-aggrandizement, Fred destroys the wall between their homes and apologizes, restoring their bond.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: While their friendship is repaired, Barney's contributions to Fibrerock remain uncredited, leaving a quiet tension about fairness and recognition.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Cost of Conformity: Fred's Moral Conflict
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Fred struggles with the ethical cost of upward mobility, torn between loyalty to his working-class roots and the demands of management. His internal conflict peaks when he fails to stop layoffs, suffers a breakdown, and is forced to flee as a fugitive.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Fred faces unemployment and mechanical failure, symbolizing his instability and vulnerability.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Fred becomes a wanted man, having failed to protect his coworkers and lost control of his public and private life.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Fred's complicity in corporate decisions haunts him, and his ability to advocate for justice remains unproven.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Kibble Crisis and Fred's Domestic Disruption
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: A seemingly minor kibble shortage spirals into a chain of domestic and professional disruptions, from Dino's overaffection to the destruction of Fred's car and eventual alienation from his home. The arc blends physical comedy, economic anxiety, and symbolic exclusion.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Fred is overwhelmed by Dino's affection due to an empty treat box, foreshadowing a larger kibble crisis.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Fred is locked out of his house by the pet saber-tooth cat, symbolizing his complete loss of domestic control and belonging.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Fred remains outside his home, physically and emotionally distanced from his family, with no clear path to reentry.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Fred's Quest for Recognition and Identity
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Fred's central goal evolves from seeking personal validation to achieving public recognition and legacy. He campaigns for leadership, embraces an executive identity, falters under temptation, but ultimately redeems himself by exposing the truth and saving his family.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Fred seeks to prove himself as a leader by running for Herdmaster and impressing the Lodge.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Fred emerges as a truth-teller and hero, exposing Lava's fraud and rescuing his family, reclaiming moral authority.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Despite his heroism, Fred's public image remains fragile, and broader societal issues like xenophobia and corporate accountability are left unresolved.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Crumbling Empire: Financial and Structural Collapse
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Mr. Slate's singular focus on expanding his housing development unravels as financial instability, supply chain issues, and internal corruption converge, threatening the collapse of his business empire.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Mr. Slate is aggressively pursuing expansion and immediate results for his development project.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He confronts systemic fraud and structural failure caused by his son's betrayal, forcing a shift from growth to damage control.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The long-term viability of Fibrerock and the stability of Slate City remain in jeopardy due to compromised materials and leadership failure.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Authority to Absence: The Erosion of Control
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Mr. Slate's public image as an unassailable authority figure gradually erodes, culminating in passive survival rather than leadership during crisis, revealing the fragility of his social dominance.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Mr. Slate wields off-screen authority used to pressure others into conformity.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He evacuates in embarrassment, stripped of agency and symbolic power as his world literally collapses around him.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His legacy and influence remain uncertain as his personal involvement in community and family diminishes despite his monumental public branding.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Exploitation and Backlash: Labor, Loyalty, and Revolt
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Mr. Slate's authoritarian labor policies spark escalating resistance, transforming employee loyalty into organized sabotage and violent confrontation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Slate asserts unchecked power by replacing adult workers with child labor and firing dissenters like Fred.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: His quarry is invaded and vandalized by a mob of former workers, marking a complete breakdown in labor relations.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The threat of further violence or systemic uprising against Slate's operations remains active and unmitigated.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Fibrerock Mirage: Innovation as Redemption and Ruin
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Mr. Slate's embrace of Fibrerock shifts from a salvaged triumph to a foundation of deception and disaster, intertwining personal ambition, corporate fraud, and technological failure.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Slate pivots from rage to excitement, seeing Fibrerock as a revolutionary asset that redeems Fred's destruction.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: The material's structural flaws are exposed due to stolen resources, revealing that the innovation was built on corruption.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The safety of all Fibrerock-based structures and accountability for the fraud remain unaddressed, risking widespread collapse.
